includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Contamination Removal Database

contaminant.name: Carbon Soot Deposits
contaminant.description: Carbon-soot contamination, it arises from incomplete combustion in engines and furnaces, thus forming black residue of fine particulates. This residue clings tightly and embeds deeply into surfaces, distinguishing itself from dust by resisting water and brushing, while on steel and concrete it alters texture and penetrates pores. Removal challenges include scratching risks and chemical residues, yet laser cleaning vaporizes soot precisely and preserves substrate integrity thus.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Carbon Monoxide, Carbon Dioxide, Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation. WARNING: Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Severe visibility reduction (60-80%), dense particulate or smoke generation
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Maintain clear evacuation routes, supplemental lighting, restrict operator movement

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Enhanced ventilation required due to dense particulate generation - 12 ACH with dual
